title: Property Definition
description: Schema for defining a property within a governed memory collection. The API accepts both the native format (propertyName, options as string, update as boolean) and the human-friendly format (name, options as array, updateSemantics as string). The human-friendly format is auto-converted on save.

id.description: Unique property identifier. Auto-generated if not provided.

propertyName.description: Human-readable property name displayed in UIs and extraction results. Alias: 'name'.

name.description: Alias for propertyName. If both are provided, propertyName takes precedence.

systemName.description: Machine-readable identifier. Lowercase, underscored. Auto-generated from propertyName if not provided. Immutable on update.

type.description: Data type for the extracted value.

description.description: Detailed description guiding the extraction model. Include scope, examples, boundaries, and edge cases. This is the most important field for extraction quality.

options.description: Allowed values when type is 'options'. API accepts both comma-separated string ('A, B, C') and array (['A', 'B', 'C']). Arrays are auto-converted to comma-separated strings on save.

autoSystem.description: If true, AI automatically populates this field during memorization. If false, field is only populated via structured writes (batch-memorize, upsert).

update.description: If true, new values REPLACE existing. If false, new values APPEND (accumulate). Alias: 'updateSemantics' with values 'replace' (= true) or 'append' (= false).

updateSemantics.description: Human-friendly alias for 'update'. 'replace' = update: true, 'append' = update: false. Auto-converted on save.

tags.description: Tags for property selection boosting. When a memorize request includes tags that match a property's tags, that property receives a +15% boost per match to its similarity score during property selection. Common tags: 'identity', 'firmographic', 'qualification', 'engagement', 'ai-extracted'.
